explanation: The natural language statement specifies the conditions under which a transition from PCV Mode to PSV Mode shall occur. It has two main parts: a general enabling condition and a specific trigger. The first part states "The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on," indicating that `ventilating` must be true for the transition to be possible. The second part provides the specific trigger: "the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI." This implies several conditions: 1) The system must currently be in PCV Mode (`PCVMode`). 2) The command to switch to PSV Mode must have been received (`PSVModeSelected`). 3) The precise moment for the transition is "at the end of a PCV inspiratory time," which translates to `inspiratoryPhaseEnd` occurring while in PCV Mode. Combining all these conditions, the LTL formula states that globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (`PCVMode`), ventilation is active (`ventilating`), the PSV mode has been selected by the user (`PSVModeSelected`), and it is the end of the inspiratory phase (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`), then in the very next state (X), the system must be in PSV Mode (`PSVMode`). This ensures the transition happens immediately upon the fulfillment of all specified preconditions and the triggering event.
output_LTL: G ( (PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode )

explanation: The natural language statement specifies the conditions under which a transition from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode must occur. The LTL property captures this by stating that globally, if the system is in PCV mode (`PCVMode`), ventilation is active (`ventilating`), a command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI (`PSVModeSelected`), and the inspiratory phase of the current PCV breath is ending (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`), then in the very next state, the system must be in PSV mode (`X PSVMode`). This combines all specified conditions (being in PCV mode, ventilation being on, the GUI command, and the end of inspiratory time) as necessary for the immediate transition to PSV mode.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode)

explanation:
The natural language statement describes two conditions for the transition from PCV Mode to PSV Mode. The translation separates these two conditions using a logical AND operator, ensuring both hold globally.

1.  "The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on": This part is captured by `G ( (PCVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode) -> ventilating )`. It states that globally, if the system is currently in PCV Mode, and it's the end of the inspiratory phase, and in the very next state it enters PSV Mode (signifying the transition from PCV to PSV), then the `ventilating` condition must be true. This ensures that any such transition only happens when ventilation is active.

2.  "the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI.": This part is captured by `G ( (PCVMode & PSVModeSelected) -> (PCVMode U (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode)) )`. It states that globally, if the system is in PCV Mode and the `PSVModeSelected` command has been received from the GUI, then the system must remain in PCV Mode until `inspiratoryPhaseEnd` occurs, and at that very moment, in the next state, it must transition into PSV Mode. The `U` (Until) operator ensures that the `PCVMode` is maintained until the specified event (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode`) occurs, precisely capturing the deferral to the "end of a PCV inspiratory time".
output_LTL: G ( ((PCVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode) -> ventilating) & ((PCVMode & PSVModeSelected) -> (PCVMode U (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & X PSVMode))) )

explanation: The LTL property states that if the system is currently in 'PCVMode' (Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode), the user has initiated a switch to PSV mode ('PSVModeSelected'), the ventilation is active ('ventilating'), and the inspiratory phase of the current breath ends ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), then in the very next state ('X'), the system must transition to 'PSVMode' (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode). This captures both conditions specified in the natural language: (1) the transition requires ventilation to be on, and (2) the transition occurs at the end of an inspiratory time when the mode change command is received from the GUI. All conditions in the antecedent must be true simultaneously for the transition to occur as specified.
output_LTL: G( (PCVMode & PSVModeSelected & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode )

explanation:
The natural language statement describes a condition under which a transition from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) mode must occur. The transition is contingent upon several conditions being met simultaneously:
1.  The system must currently be in PCV Mode (`PCVMode`).
2.  Ventilation must be active (`ventilating`).
3.  The specific timing for the transition is 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time', which is captured by the atomic proposition `inspiratoryPhaseEnd`.
4.  The trigger for the transition is 'if the change mode command has been received from the GUI', which is represented by `PSVModeSelected`.

Combining these conditions, the LTL formula states that 'Globally (G), if the system is in PCV Mode (`PCVMode`) AND ventilation is active (`ventilating`) AND it is the end of the inspiratory phase (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`) AND the PSV mode has been selected by the user (`PSVModeSelected`), THEN in the Next state (X), the system must be in PSV Mode (`PSVMode`).'

The `X` (next) operator is used because the phrase 'at the end of a PCV inspiratory time' implies an immediate consequence in the subsequent state once all triggering conditions are met at the current state.
output_LTL: G (PCVMode & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected -> X PSVMode)

explanation: The property captures the conditions under which a transition from PCV mode to PSV mode must occur. It states that globally (G), if the system is currently operating in PCV mode (PCVMode), and ventilation is active (ventilating), and a command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI (PSVModeSelected), and simultaneously the current inspiratory phase is ending (inspiratoryPhaseEnd), then the system must transition to PSV mode in the very next state (X PSVMode). This translation ensures that all specified conditions – being in PCV mode, ventilation being on, the GUI command for PSV, and the end of the inspiratory time – must collectively be true for the mode transition to occur immediately.
output_LTL: G ( (PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode )

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PCV mode (`PCVMode`) and ventilation is active (`ventilating`), then upon the simultaneous occurrence of the end of the inspiratory phase (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`) and the reception of a change mode command to PSV from the GUI (`PSVModeSelected`), the system shall transition to PSV mode in the very next state (`X PSVMode`). This formulation captures that being in PCV mode and having ventilation active are preconditions for this specific transition, and the combined event of `inspiratoryPhaseEnd` and `PSVModeSelected` serves as the precise trigger for the transition.
output_LTL: G(PCVMode & ventilating & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected -> X PSVMode)

explanation: The LTL formula captures the specified transition conditions for PCV Mode to PSV Mode. It states that globally (G), if the system is currently in 'PCVMode', and 'ventilating' is active, and a 'PSVModeSelected' command (change mode command from the GUI) has been received, and the 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' has been reached (end of a PCV inspiratory time), then in the very next state (X), the system must transition into 'PSVMode'. This comprehensively addresses both parts of the natural language statement: that the transition occurs if ventilation is on, and specifically, at the end of PCV inspiratory time when the GUI command is received. The 'X' operator ensures the immediate transition upon these conditions being met.
output_LTL: G((PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode)

explanation: The LTL property captures the requirement that a transition from PCV mode to PSV mode must occur under specific conditions. The natural language states that "The transition from PCV to PSV shall occur if ventilation is on, the transition from PCV to PSV shall occur at the end of a PCV inspiratory time if the change mode command has been received from the GUI." This is interpreted as a single set of conditions that must be met for the transition to take place. Therefore, the property states that globally (G), if ventilation is on (`ventilating`), and we are currently in PCV mode (`PCVMode`), and it is the end of the inspiratory phase (`inspiratoryPhaseEnd`), and the command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI (`PSVModeSelected`), then in the very next state (`X`), the system must be in PSV mode (`PSVMode`). This ensures that the transition happens as specified, from PCV to PSV, only when all the stated conditions are simultaneously met.
output_LTL: G((ventilating & PCVMode & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & PSVModeSelected) -> X PSVMode)

explanation: The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PCV mode ('PCVMode'), ventilation is active ('ventilating'), a user command to switch to PSV mode has been received from the GUI ('PSVModeSelected'), and the inspiratory phase is ending ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), then in the very next state (X), the system must be in PSV mode ('PSVMode'). This captures the requirement for the transition from PCV to PSV to occur precisely at the end of a PCV inspiratory time, provided ventilation is on and the mode change command has been issued.
output_LTL: G ((PCVMode & ventilating & PSVModeSelected & inspiratoryPhaseEnd) -> X PSVMode)
